executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: The marketing owner loop still does not have a pass-worthy independent verification artifact because primary Codeberg adoption remains flat inside a live measurement window, so the architecture verifier correctly stays fail-closed.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Keep pressure inside the marketing owner loop: either produce measurable primary-repo movement or expire and replace the current distribution tactic when the measurement window closes.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus jobs.json comparison with explicit live-vs-persisted separation

owns: bounded timeout reruns and verifier refreshes
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Marketing independent verification is still legitimately red
findings.mechanism: The marketing loop runner is now operationally healthy, but the independent verifier still refuses certification because Codeberg adoption has not moved within the active measurement window.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence is still missing for the owner loop that owns primary-repo adoption.
findings.recommended_fix: Do not certify around the watchpoint. Hold the red state until the current measurement window either produces Codeberg movement or is replaced by a new tactic.

findings.title: Marketing runner/runtime health was self-poisoning on verifier failures
findings.mechanism: The runner bundle had been executing the independent verifier and verifier inside the runtime bundle, which made the runner artifact go red whenever certification stayed red.
findings.root_cause: Operational execution and certification were coupled in the same runner contract.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep runtime production scripts in the runner bundle and leave certification to the dedicated verifier surfaces.

findings.title: Architecture fail-closed reporting had escalation noise mixed into blocker text
findings.mechanism: Derived escalation issues were being counted alongside the root marketing blocker, which diluted blocker localization without changing the live verdict.
findings.root_cause: Architecture verifier surfaces were not collapsing repeat-escalation artifacts away from the underlying blocker list.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep escalation artifacts available for history, but localize architecture blocker text to the root non-self, non-escalation issue set.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep the architecture verdict pinned to the single live owner-loop blocker
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The stack is fail-closed for a real reason, so noise must not outrank the actual blocker.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Architecture red remains accurate and compact: marketing certification stays the blocker until it genuinely turns green.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Force outcome movement or tactic replacement inside the marketing owner loop
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Primary Codeberg adoption is still flat and is the only blocker still preventing green signoff.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The next architecture green can only come from a real marketing pass artifact, not from wording or verifier churn.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Removed the marketing independent verifier and verifier from marketing_loop_runner.py so the runner artifact now reflects runtime execution health instead of certification state.
Updated the architecture independent verifier and verifier to ignore escalation-only health artifacts when summarizing live blockers.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification found architecture blockers that prevent a healthy verdict.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh marketing independent pass after the current primary-repo adoption measurement window either produces Codeberg movement or is replaced with a new tactic.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Primary Codeberg adoption is still flat under a live measurement window
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: This is now the only root blocker still keeping the architecture verifier red.

architecture_verifier_errors: health monitor reports non-architecture live issues: marketing_independent_verification:loop_verification_fail

errors: health monitor reports non-architecture live issues: marketing_independent_verification:loop_verification_fail
